SignalGateCommand.docs: CQRS command for the signal-gate chain-sequencing use case. Carries the gate label, items directory, and active track id. Created pre-baseline; action add.

SignalGateOutput.docs: Output DTO for the signal-gate use case. Aggregated result of the 4-chain sequencing. Consumed by cli_driver render layer.

SignalChainOutput.docs: Per-chain output DTO inside a signal-gate run. One entry per SoT-chain evaluated. `stdout`/`stderr` are opaque output strings produced by the chain verifier; they are free-text and not domain-constrained (docs justify raw String per R9).

SignalGateError.docs: Error type for the SignalGateService. ChainExecutionFailed: one chain verifier returned an error. InvalidTrackId: track id could not be resolved. StrictnessConfigLoad: signal-gates config could not be loaded.

SignalGateInteractor.docs: Interactor implementing SignalGateService. Holds the injected SignalLayerReader port, SignalGateMatrix strictness source, and chain-runner ports (AdrChainRunnerPort, SpecAdrChainRunnerPort, LayerChainRunnerPort) as private fields. Sequences the 4 SoT chains (adr-user, spec-adr, catalog-spec, impl-catalog), resolves strictness per gate matrix, and aggregates results.

SpecAdrSignalCommand.docs: CQRS command for the spec-ADR signal calculation use case. Carries the path to the spec.json to be read, evaluated, and re-written.

SpecAdrSignalOutput.docs: Output DTO for the spec-ADR signal calculation use case. Returns the aggregate blue/yellow/red signal counts after writing the updated spec.json. Counts are unsigned integers; they are truly opaque counters with no domain constraints beyond non-negativity (R9: u32 justified as domain-agnostic counter value).

SpecAdrSignalError.docs: Error type for the SpecAdrSignalService. All variants carry String payloads for usecase purity (no std::io::Error). Read: I/O failure reading spec.json. Decode: JSON/domain decode failure. Encode: encode failure. Write: atomic-write failure. Infrastructure adapter maps concrete errors to String at the boundary.

ArchivedTrackTelemetryError.docs: Error type for ArchivedTrackTelemetryPort. Io: filesystem failure creating the telemetry file or directory. Serialize: JSON serialization failure.

ArchivedTrackTelemetryCommand.docs: CQRS command for the archived-track telemetry use case. `subcommand` is an opaque CLI subcommand label recorded as free text; it is not a domain value object.

ArchivedTrackTelemetryInteractor.docs: Interactor implementing ArchivedTrackTelemetryService. Holds the injected ArchivedTrackTelemetryPort as a private field and delegates telemetry persistence through that secondary port, keeping cli_driver from invoking infrastructure adapters directly.

SpecAdrSignalInteractor.docs: Interactor implementing SpecAdrSignalService. Holds injected file ports as private fields. Executes the read-domain-document->evaluate->set->write-domain-document cycle for spec-ADR signal persistence; JSON decode/encode stays inside the SpecFileWriterPort adapter, extracted from cli_composition per ADR 1328 D4.

D4OrchestrationError.docs: Shared error type for the D4 orchestration extraction services added by this track. String payloads are opaque diagnostic text mapped at the usecase boundary and rendered by cli_driver; they are not domain value objects.

DryFragmentPipelineCommand.docs: CQRS command for the D4 diff-fragment pipeline used by the fixpoint dry gate. `canonical_root` is the project root used for fragment extraction; `repo_root` is the git repository root used for explicit diff discovery and path normalization; `base` is the diff base commit. The interactor receives `repo_root` explicitly and does not require caller CWD changes.

DryFragmentPipelineOutput.docs: Output DTO for the D4 dry fragment pipeline. Carries derived FragmentRefs as a BTreeSet plus legacy records_* compatibility fields. For this service the records_* fields are fixed at 0; record-count aggregation remains in the dry_write path.

DryFragmentPipelineInteractor.docs: Interactor implementing DryFragmentPipelineService. Extracts the build_current_fragment_refs multi-step orchestration from cli_composition into usecase application logic. The dry_check_approved code path retains only a small one-line loop that calls the usecase free function `fragment_ref_of` per fragment — that is a single-step usecase API call, not multi-step orchestration, so it remains in cli_composition by design. The broader dry-gate orchestration (the dry-gate enabled/disabled branching in fixpoint_resolve) is tracked by T008. dry_write record-count aggregation stays in the dry-write path; DryFragmentPipelineOutput records_* counters remain 0 for catalogue compatibility. Private dependencies use the dry-check diff source and the new CodeFragmentExtractorPort; CodeFragmentExtractorAdapter is the infrastructure adapter edge for fragment extraction.

FixpointDryGateCommand.docs: CQRS command for the fixpoint-resolve dry-gate orchestration extracted by D4. The boolean mirrors the existing dry-check enablement switch and is application configuration, not a domain concept.

FixpointDryGateOutput.docs: Output DTO for the fixpoint dry-gate orchestration. `summary` is opaque free text for cli_driver rendering and is not a domain value object.

FixpointDryGateInteractor.docs: Interactor implementing FixpointDryGateService. Owns the dry-gate branching, CWD guard, corpus-root manifest branching, and DryCheckApprovalInteractor construction currently embedded in cli_composition.

PrReviewPollingCommand.docs: CQRS command for the PR review polling application service. `repository` is an opaque repository label supplied by the CLI path and is rendered as free text.

PrReviewPollingOutput.docs: Output DTO for the PR review polling loop. `summary` is opaque free text for cli_driver rendering.

PrReviewPollingInteractor.docs: Interactor implementing PrReviewPollingService. Extracts the PR review polling loop from cli_composition into a usecase application service with injected review-provider ports.

SignalGateService.docs: Application service (primary port) for the signal-gate chain sequencing use case. Implementations sequence the 4 SoT chains and aggregate outcomes. Extracted from cli_composition signal_check_gate per ADR 1328 D4.

SpecAdrSignalService.docs: Application service (primary port) for the spec-ADR signal calculation and persistence use case. Runs the read-domain-document->evaluate->set->write-domain-document cycle; JSON decode/encode stays behind the SpecFileWriterPort adapter boundary. Extracted from cli_composition signal_calc_spec_adr per ADR 1328 D4.

ArchivedTrackTelemetryService.docs: Application service (primary port) for archived-track telemetry emission. cli_driver invokes this usecase service; the interactor delegates persistence through ArchivedTrackTelemetryPort so the secondary adapter stays behind the usecase boundary.

DryFragmentPipelineService.docs: Application service (primary port) for D4 diff-fragment pipeline extraction. Covers the build_current_fragment_refs multi-step orchestration moved out of cli_composition by T007. The dry_check_approved code path retains only a small one-line loop that calls the usecase free function `fragment_ref_of` per fragment — that is a single-step usecase API call, not multi-step orchestration, and remains in cli_composition by design. The broader dry-gate orchestration in fixpoint_resolve is tracked by T008. dry_write record-count aggregation stays in the dry-write path; DryFragmentPipelineOutput records_* counters remain 0 for catalogue compatibility.

FixpointDryGateService.docs: Application service (primary port) for D4 fixpoint-resolve dry-gate orchestration. Owns the dry-gate branch and related application sequencing moved out of cli_composition by T008.

PrReviewPollingService.docs: Application service (primary port) for D4 PR review polling. Extracts the polling loop from cli_composition so cli_driver invokes a single usecase service.

SpecFileWriterPort.docs: Secondary port for reading and atomically writing spec.json files. Operates on domain::SpecDocument — the infrastructure adapter performs JSON codec internally (decode on read, encode + atomic write on write). This keeps the usecase interactor free of serde_json and infrastructure::spec::codec. Decode/Encode error variants on SpecAdrSignalError are returned by the adapter when codec fails.

ReviewGateStatePort.docs: Baseline port referenced so that FsReviewGateStateAdapter -.impl.-> ReviewGateStatePort edge is visible in the contract-map (R7).

RefVerifyGateStatePort.docs: Baseline port referenced so that FsRefVerifyGateStateAdapter -.impl.-> RefVerifyGateStatePort edge is visible in the contract-map (R7).

DryCheckAgentPort.docs: Baseline port referenced so that RecordingDryAgent -.impl.-> DryCheckAgentPort edge is visible in the contract-map (R7).

DryCheckApprovalService.docs: Baseline service port referenced so that NoOpDryApprovalService -.impl.-> DryCheckApprovalService edge is visible in the contract-map (R7).

SemanticIndexPort.docs: Baseline port referenced so that NullInsertIndexProxy -.impl.-> SemanticIndexPort and NoopSemanticIndexPort -.impl.-> SemanticIndexPort edges are visible in the contract-map (R7).

BranchReaderPort.docs: Baseline port referenced so that the LazyBranchReader delete entry is traceable in the contract-map (R7). LazyBranchReader is eliminated; SystemGitRepo directly implements this port.

ArchivedTrackTelemetryPort.docs: Secondary port for emitting a telemetry event when an archived-track subcommand is dispatched. Abstracts the direct std::fs / serde_json / chrono I/O that previously lived in apps/cli/src/main.rs:247-294. The infrastructure adapter owns timestamp capture and receives the telemetry directory at construction.

AdrChainRunnerPort.docs: Secondary port for chain ⓪ (ADR → user): runs the ADR signal scan for a given project root and strictness setting, returning a chain output. The infrastructure adapter runs execute_verify_adr_signals_with_strict internally; the port exposes only the rendered text outcome.

SpecAdrChainRunnerPort.docs: Secondary port for chain ① (spec → ADR): reads and evaluates the spec.json signal gate with the resolved strictness, returning a chain output. The infrastructure adapter runs verify_from_spec_json + resolve_trusted_root internally; the port exposes only the rendered text outcome.

LayerChainRunnerPort.docs: Secondary port for chains ② and ③ (catalog-spec / impl-catalog): runs the argless layer-chain verifier for a given chain label and per-layer parameters. Abstracts signal_check_layer_chain_with_strict composition so the interactor can sequence all four chains without importing infrastructure.

CodeFragmentExtractorPort.docs: Secondary port for extracting code fragments from a workspace root. Abstracts infrastructure::semantic_dup::extractor::extract_code_fragments so the DryFragmentPipelineInteractor does not import from infrastructure directly. The infrastructure adapter (CodeFragmentExtractorAdapter) wraps extract_code_fragments and maps ExtractError to String at the boundary.

DryCheckDiffSource.docs: Dry-check secondary port for diff hunk listing. T007 modified signature to add `repo_root: &Path` parameter (was previously CWD-dependent via SystemGitRepo::discover()). The adapter now uses SystemGitRepo::discover_from(repo_root) explicitly, removing ambient CWD dependency from the usecase orchestration boundary.
